_provenance: F. prausnitzii / F. duncaniae A2-165 chassis profile, encoding genomic + engineering-toolkit + physiology properties that bear on heterologous expression feasibility.
host: Faecalibacterium prausnitzii A2-165 (now F. duncaniae per Sakamoto 2022 IJSEM, doi:10.1099/ijsem.0.005379)

taxonomy.type_strain: A2-165 = JCM 31915 = DSM 17677 = ATCC BAA-? (laboratory model strain since 2002 isolation)
taxonomy.current_species_name: F. duncaniae (Sakamoto 2022); historically named F. prausnitzii A2-165

genome.source: Fraccascia 2022 Microbiol Resour Announc doi:10.1128/mra.00824-22 — 8 healthy-stool Faecalibacterium genomes
genome.verification_note: GC 56.6% pre-commit grep-verified from Fraccascia 2022 PMC9872689 line 11 of content.lines

physiology.oxygen_tolerance: Strict obligate anaerobe (dies under aerobic conditions within minutes; though oxygen-tolerance under riboflavin/cysteine conditions has been characterized — extracellular electron transfer via riboflavin)
physiology.preferred_growth_environment: Colonic lumen (effectively anoxic, ~0% O2 partial pressure)
physiology.growth_in_lab: Requires anaerobic chamber + yeast-extract-casein-fatty-acid broth (YCFA) + acetate; doubling time ~3-5 hr under optimal anaerobic conditions
physiology.native_butyrate_production: Yes — via butyryl-CoA:acetate CoA-transferase pathway (Louis 2009 doi:10.1111/j.1462-2920.2009.02066.x). One of the dominant butyrate producers in human colon (3-15% of healthy microbiota).
physiology.obligate_anaerobe_implications: Any heterologous payload requiring O2 as a substrate (uricase, lactoferrin-bacteriostatic-action partial, complement-tailoring P450s) is INTRINSICALLY mismatched with this chassis.

genetic_toolkit_maturity_2026_05.documented_status: F. prausnitzii is GENETIC-MANIPULATION-NAIVE as of 2026-05. The 2023 Sheridan et al. PMC10696588 review of 'novel strains of human gut bacteria' covers Bifidobacterium, Bacteroides, Roseburia, Eubacterium rectale, Butyrivibrio fibrisolvens — but does NOT include F. prausnitzii in its established-strains table. The 2023 Martín et al. FEMS Microbiol Rev PMID 37451743 (the definitive recent Faecalibacterium review) names the genus as a next-generation-probiotic (NGP) and live-biotherapeutic-product (LBP) candidate but documents zero engineering precedent.
genetic_toolkit_maturity_2026_05.delivery_workaround_in_literature: MAM (Microbial Anti-inflammatory Molecule) — the canonical anti-inflammatory protein produced by F. prausnitzii — was delivered to mouse gut via Lactococcus lactis carrying a plasmid (Quévrain 2016 Gut doi:10.1136/gutjnl-2014-307649; Breyner 2017 Front Microbiol doi:10.3389/fmicb.2017.00114). i.e. when researchers wanted to engineer F. prausnitzii's effector molecule INTO the gut, they did not engineer F. prausnitzii itself — they used L. lactis as a delivery surrogate. This is the strongest single piece of evidence that F. prausnitzii's genetic toolkit is non-trivially behind that of L. lactis / E. coli Nissle / Bacteroides.
genetic_toolkit_maturity_2026_05.phylogenetic_neighbor_toolkits_inheritab: Roseburia inulinivorans and Eubacterium rectale (close Lachnospiraceae relatives) have a conjugation toolkit using Tn1545/TnK10 conjugative transposons + tet(W)/tet(O) selection markers (Scott 2008 Appl Environ Microbiol doi:10.1128/AEM.02807-07; Sheridan 2019 Anaerobe doi:10.1016/j.anaerobe.2019.06.008; Sheridan 2020 Bio Protoc doi:10.21769/BioProtoc.3575). These protocols are plausibly adaptable to F. prausnitzii but adaptation has NOT been published. Adapting an obligate-anaerobe conjugation protocol from a closely related species is the path most likely to succeed; budget 1-2 years of focused PhD-level work.

engineering_complexity_factors.selection_marker_availability: Limited — tet(W) tetW from related Lachnospiraceae plausibly works; cmR / kanR established for E. coli might fail (no cytosolic studies); erythromycin (ermB) common in Firmicutes
engineering_complexity_factors.restriction_modification_systems: F. prausnitzii A2-165 genome encodes Type II RM systems — PAM (plasmid artificial modification) per Yasui 2009 doi:10.1128/AEM.02418-08 may be needed (10,000-fold improvement in Bifidobacterium adolescentis with this method)
engineering_complexity_factors.secretion_pathways_present: Sec-translocon: YES (SecA/SecYEG conserved in all Firmicutes — F. prausnitzii has annotated SecA/SecY). Tat-translocon: PRESENT in F. prausnitzii (canonical TatABC genes found in genome annotation, though specifics not separately confirmed in this analysis). Native signal peptides documented for MAM secretion (Quévrain 2016).

fitness_considerations.host_lifestyle_metabolic_budget: Butyrate production is a SIGNIFICANT carbon-flux commitment for F. prausnitzii. Diverting metabolic budget to heterologous payload expression risks fitness loss, especially under colonic-luminal competition. Native-payload overexpression (butyryl-CoA:acetate CoA-transferase) has the lowest fitness cost.
fitness_considerations.MAM_native_secretion_baseline: F. prausnitzii natively secretes MAM and roughly 7 other proteomically identified peptides. This establishes that the host CAN secrete soluble protein at biologically relevant levels — but heterologous secretion titers have never been measured.
